sections.subtitle: Current Form
sections.text: Espanyol are struggling, having lost their last 4 matches and failing to win in their last 6, with defensive issues evident as they've not kept a clean sheet in 7 games. Celta Vigo are also in a rough patch, without a win in their last 4 matches, though they've shown resilience with draws and a recent win. Both teams have been involved in high-scoring games, with both teams scoring in 4 of their last 5 matches.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: In their last two encounters, Espanyol have dominated Celta Vigo, winning both matches (1-0 and 2-0) with solid defensive displays. Historically, Espanyol have won 3 of the last 5 head-to-heads, indicating a psychological edge going into this match.

sections.subtitle: Season Statistics
sections.text: Espanyol, sitting 6th with 34 points, have a decent attack (27 goals scored) but struggle with possession (18th in the league at 42.5%) and defensive solidity (31 goals conceded). Celta Vigo, just behind in 7th with 33 points, have a slightly better goal difference (30 scored, 25 conceded) and often perform well in the final stages of matches, scoring frequently late in games.

sections.subtitle: Key Players
sections.text: For Espanyol, Pere Milla (6 goals) and Edu Expósito (5 assists) will be crucial in breaking down Celta's defense. Celta Vigo will rely on Borja Iglesias (9 goals, 2 assists) to exploit Espanyol's shaky backline, especially given his recent form.

sections.subtitle: Tactical Insights
sections.text: Espanyol’s strength lies in headers (league-leading 10 goals) and accurate crosses (2nd in the league), which could target Celta’s aerial weakness (17th in aerial duels won). Celta Vigo, with better possession stats in recent games, may look to control the midfield and exploit Espanyol’s tendency to concede first (5 of last 7 matches).

sections.subtitle: Match Prediction
sections.text: Given Celta Vigo’s slightly better recent form and Sofascore’s predicted probabilities (44% away win vs. 26% home win), they are the slight favorites. Espanyol’s home struggles and defensive frailties might be the deciding factor, though their head-to-head edge could make it close.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Half-time overview
halftimeSections.text: At half-time, Celta Vigo leads Espanyol 1-0 at the RCDE Stadium in Cornella. The solitary goal came from Ferran Jutglà in the 38th minute, showcasing Celta Vigo's ability to capitalize on their opportunities. Espanyol, despite having a slight edge in possession and more entries into the final third, failed to convert their chances, missing a big opportunity in the first half. Celta Vigo, on the other hand, demonstrated clinical finishing and a stronger expected goals output, reflecting their efficiency in attack.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Team performance breakdown
halftimeSections.text: Espanyol has shown intent with frequent entries into the penalty area and a balanced share of possession. However, their inability to convert big chances into goals has left them trailing. Their defensive structure has held relatively firm, but they struggled to win aerial duels, which could be a concern against a physically dominant Celta Vigo side. Celta Vigo, while slightly behind in possession, made the most of their fewer entries into the final third by creating more significant scoring opportunities. Their superior duel-winning ability, especially in the air, has given them an edge in maintaining control during key moments.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Key player contributions
halftimeSections.text: For Espanyol, players like Cyril Ngonge have been pivotal in creating opportunities, showing creativity in the final third. Roberto Fernández and Carlos Romero also posed threats, though they couldn’t find the net. On the Celta Vigo side, Ferran Jutglà stands out with his well-taken goal, proving to be the difference-maker. Javier Rueda’s assist and involvement in build-up play were crucial, while Marcos Alonso’s presence in defense and attack added balance to their performance. Both teams have players capable of changing the game’s complexion in the second half.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Attack momentum analysis
halftimeSections.text: The first half saw Espanyol dominate the early stages with consistent attacking spells, pushing forward with intent in the opening minutes. Their momentum was particularly strong in patches, especially around the middle of the half, where they created sustained pressure. However, Celta Vigo shifted the tide with a strong attacking phase leading up to their goal, gaining momentum and confidence thereafter. Towards the end of the half, Celta maintained a series of attacking moves, while Espanyol struggled to regain their earlier rhythm, though they did show a late push just before the whistle.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Tactical observations
halftimeSections.text: Espanyol’s approach has leaned heavily on exploiting the final third through frequent entries, but their lack of precision in front of goal has been a glaring issue. They may need to adjust their finishing or bring on more attacking dynamism in the second half to break through Celta’s defense. Celta Vigo has played a more measured game, focusing on efficiency rather than volume in attack. Their ability to win duels and control aerial battles has allowed them to disrupt Espanyol’s rhythm, and they could look to exploit counter-attacking opportunities in the second half to extend their lead.
